title: 10 best herbal decoctions for pimples
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for pimples are concentrated liquid extracts made by steeping herbs in hot water, used to treat acne-prone skin. They work by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and regulating hormonal imbalances that can contribute to pimple formation. Examples of effective herbal decoctions include neem, tea tree, and turmeric, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and clear the skin. By using these decoctions, individuals with acne-prone skin can enjoy clearer, smoother complexions and increased confidence in their appearance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with pimples because its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling. The antibacterial compounds in sage also help to eliminate acne-causing bacteria, preventing clogged pores and breakouts. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and acne. As a result, incorporating sage decoctions into your skincare routine can lead to clearer, healthier-looking skin and a reduced appearance of pimples.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage decoctions contain compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to acne development.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in sage decoctions have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Antioxidant properties: Sage decoctions are rich in antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid, which can help neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells and contribute to acne development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with pimples.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like camphor and bornyl acetate, rhyzomes help to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried or 40-60 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves with one liter of boiling water.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes to allow the herbal mixture to steep and infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating it for up to three days or freezing it for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with pimples because it possesses natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce redness. The antiseptic compounds in lemon balm also help to unclog pores, allowing for a clearer complexion. Additionally, the soothing effects of lemon balm can calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a smoother appearance. By incorporating lemon balm decoctions into one's skincare routine, individuals may experience a significant reduction in pimple size and severity, leading to healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial activity: Lemon balm's antibacterial properties help reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which is a common cause of acne and pimple formation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and rosmarinic acid present in lemon balm decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples.

Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development, promoting healthy skin and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and other nutrients that promote healthy skin cell regeneration and minimize acne scars.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried melissa officinalis leaves measuring one teaspoon per cup water.
Boil fresh spring or filtered water in a saucepan for 10 minutes to ensure sterility.
Add the dried melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled melissa officinalis decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with pimples because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in calendula, the herb from which pot marigold is derived, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial overgrowth that can lead to acne. Additionally, the decoction's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and promoting faster healing of pimples. By using pot marigold decoctions topically, individuals can effectively address pimple-prone skin concerns and achieve a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and pimple inflammation.

Antibacterial properties: The decoction's antibacterial properties, specifically its ability to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to the development of pimples.

Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne and pimple formation, and also reduce the appearance of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) associated with acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, the roots of the pot marigold plant help to reduce inflammation and soothe acne-prone skin.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, the seeds of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm redness and irritation associated with pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Measure out 10 grams of the ground calendula and combine it with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 30 minutes before straining the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and transfer the clear liquid decoction to a sterilized dark glass bottle for storage in the refrigerator.
Take 20 milliliters of the cooled calendula decoction orally twice daily as a tea, or use topically after mixing with a carrier oil for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with pimples because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling. The antimicrobial compounds in aloe vera also help to combat bacteria that can cause acne, preventing further breakouts. Additionally, the natural antibacterial agents in aloe vera can reduce inflammation and promote healing, helping to clear up pimples quickly. Overall, aloe decoctions offer a gentle yet effective way to address acne-prone skin without harsh chemicals or drying agents.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne, helping to soothe and calm the skin.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in herbal aloe decoctions can help kill bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that contribute to the development of pimples, reducing the risk of infection and preventing future breakouts.

Wound-healing properties: Herbal aloe decoctions contain compounds that promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and enhancing tissue repair, which can help reduce acne scarring and promote faster recovery from pimple lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in aloin and other bioactive compounds, which help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections associated with pimples.
Roots: Containing saponins and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties to soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature plants weighing at least 2-3 pounds each.
Mix 2 tablespoons of dried calendula flowers with 4 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Combine 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel with 2 cups of cooled calendula decoction and stir well.
Store the herbal decoction in an amber glass bottle refrigerated for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with pimples because its bitter compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxadiol, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The decoction's antiseptic qualities also help to combat bacterial infections that can cause breakouts, while its diuretic properties promote healthy digestion and liver function, which is essential for removing toxins from the body that can contribute to acne formation. By incorporating dandelion decoctions into your skincare routine, you can effectively address pimple-causing issues from the inside out.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and lutein, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to inflammation, thereby reducing pimple severity.

Anti-bacterial properties: The decoction of dandelion roots and leaves has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria commonly found in the pores of acne-prone skin, which can help prevent infection and reduce inflammation associated with pimples.

Lymphatic stimulant: Dandelion's bitter compounds have been traditionally used to stimulate lymphatic circulation, helping to remove toxins and excess fluids from the skin, which can alleviate acne symptoms by reducing congestion and preventing blockages.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne-causing bacteria and redness associated with pimples.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale root and add it to 250ml of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herb to infuse its active compounds into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature, then refrigerate it at 2-8°c for up to 24 hours before use.
Use 1/4 cup of the cooled decoction as a face toner twice daily, gently massaging it onto affected areas and allowing it to dry before applying sunscreen or moisturizer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium decoctions helps with pimples because they possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties that effectively combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce redness and swelling. The decoction's ability to balance the skin's natural pH levels also prevents excessive oil production, clogging pores, and promoting a healthy skin environment. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Geranium's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes, which can cause acne and pimples by preventing the spread of infection and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as geraniin and rosmarinic acid, help to soothe redness, swelling, and itching associated with pimples, thereby reducing their appearance and alleviating discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Geranium's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the development of acne, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild geranium plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Barks: The barks of the wild geranium plant contain antiseptic compounds that aid in killing bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried geranium maculatum leaves and flowers at dawn or dusk.
Combine the herbs with two cups of boiling water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Reduce heat to simmer for 15-20 minutes allowing the mixture to steep and infuse.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower contains compounds like cichoric acid and echinacein, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, making it effective in soothing acne-prone skin and reducing redness.

Antimicrobial properties: The decoction's antimicrobial activity helps combat bacterial overgrowth on the skin, a common cause of pimples, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that can contribute to acne.

Immunomodulatory properties: Kansas Coneflower's immunomodulating compounds may help regulate the immune response in the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing excessive production of sebum, which can lead to clogged pores and pimples.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in acne-prone skin.
Barks: The barks of the Kansas coneflower plant contain compounds that help to reduce bacterial growth and combat infections, making it an effective ingredient in treating pimples.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately two teaspoons of dried echinacea angustifolia root for every eight ounces of water.
Combine measured echinacea angustifolia root and water in a medium-sized pot.
Heat the mixture over high heat, bringing it to a boil, then reduce the heat to low simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze for later use.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, can effectively reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, the decoction's antibacterial activity helps combat P. acnes, a common bacteria that causes breakouts. By reducing inflammation and combating bacterial growth, Kansas coneflower decoctions provide a natural and effective way to help clear up pimples and promote healthy, clear skin.

American witch hazel decoctions helps with pimples because of its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The decoction's high concentration of tannins and flavonoids helps to reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne, while its antimicrobial properties combat the bacteria that cause breakouts. Additionally, witch hazel's natural astringent properties help to dry out pimples and reduce their appearance, leaving skin looking clearer and more balanced. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from acne without harsh chemicals or synthetic ingredients.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Witch Hazel's antimicrobial compounds, such as tannins and phenolic acids, help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne and inflammation, thereby reducing pimple severity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch Hazel's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and glycosides, help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples, making them less noticeable and more manageable.

Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent compounds, such as tannins, help to dry out the skin and shrink the size of pores, reducing the appearance of blackheads and whiteheads that can contribute to pimple formation.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are used because they contain high amounts of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Roots: The roots are used due to their rich content of saponins, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help combat acne-causing bacteria and fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in early summer morning for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cold water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine one tablespoon of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers with two cups of boiling water in a ceramic pot.
Simmer the mixture for 20-25 minutes, then strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator at 40°f (4°c) to maintain its potency and shelf life for up to five days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry decoctions helps with pimples because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and astringent properties. The decoction's potent antioxidant capacity helps neutralize free radicals that can cause skin damage and inflammation, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, the astringent properties help dry out excess oil and shrink pores, reducing the appearance of pimples and preventing new ones from forming. As a result, highbush cranberry decoctions offer a natural and effective solution for soothing and clearing up acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry decoctions contain powerful antioxidants that help combat free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which can contribute to pimple formation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help reduce redness, swelling, and acne lesions by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antibacterial properties: Highbush cranberry's antibacterial compounds, including coumarins and phenolic acids, can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, highbush cranberry roots can help reduce inflammation and prevent further breakouts.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, the barks have astringent and antimicrobial properties that can help dry out acne lesions and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and flowers per cup of water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a clean glass container or teapot.
Pour boiling filtered water over the mixture to ensure optimal extraction time.
Allow the decoction to steep for 10-15 minutes before straining and cooling.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with pimples because its mucilaginous properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with acne. The antioxidant-rich compounds in marshmallow root also help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to pimple formation. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory effects may help to reduce sebum production, unclog pores, and prevent bacteria from thriving, ultimately leading to a clearer and more balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow decoction's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it a soothing treatment for pimple-prone skin.
Antibacterial: The decoction's antibacterial properties combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that can cause pimples, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy skin.
Emollient: Marshmallow's emollient property helps to soften and moisturize the skin, unclogging pores and preventing clogged pores from forming, which can reduce the appearance of acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm inflamed skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain antioxidants that combat acne-causing free radicals and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis leaves or flowers and rinse them gently with cold water.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried herb or 2 tablespoons of fresh herb with 1 cup boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herb's active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before storing it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort decoctions helps with pimples because its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which can contribute to acne. Additionally, St John's Wort has antimicrobial effects that target Propionibacterium acnes bacteria, a common cause of pimples. As the decoction is applied topically, it helps soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting clearer complexion and reducing the appearance of acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds like hyperforin, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antibacterial properties: The decoction's antimicrobial compounds, such as hypericin, can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to acne development.

Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort decoctions contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may protect the skin from oxidative stress and environmental damage that can lead to acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: St. John's Wort roots have been used for centuries to treat skin issues, including acne and pimples, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Combine one tablespoon of dried or two tablespoons of fresh herbs with two cups of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Remove from heat and let steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and reserve for later use.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store it in an amber glass bottle with tight-fitting lid in the refrigerator for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The decoction's antibacterial compounds also target P.acnes bacteria, a common cause of pimples, thereby preventing further breakouts. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant capabilities help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin regeneration. As a result, using stinging nettle decoctions as a natural remedy for acne can lead to clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle decoctions contain compounds that reduce inflammation, which helps to calm redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in nettle decoctions neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne, reducing the appearance of pimples.
Astringent: Nettle decoctions have astringent properties that help to dry up excess oil and reduce the size of pores, making it harder for bacteria to multiply and cause acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Nettle roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Seeds: Nettle seeds contain omega-3 fatty acids, which can help combat acne-causing bacteria and soothe skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems for use in the decoction.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris then chop it coarsely.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a pot and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool completely then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with pimples because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling caused by acne. The decoction also contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that inhibits the production of hormones that stimulate oil production in the skin, leading to clogged pores and acne. Additionally, licorice root has antiseptic properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause pimples. By reducing inflammation, regulating oil production, and combating bacteria, herbal licorice decoctions provide a natural solution for clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice decoctions have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of pimples.

Soilung (Saponin) content: Licorice root contains a type of saponin called soilung, which has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective against P. acnes bacteria that can cause acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in reducing pimple inflammation and preventing bacterial growth.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like licoricidin and licorisoflavan A, which have been shown to exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to soothe and treat pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 15-20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and rinse it thoroughly with clean water.
Boil 2 liters of filtered water in a large saucepan for 10 minutes before adding the roots.
Add the rinsed roots to the boiling water, reduce heat, and simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to stand at room temperature for 24 hours before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon grass decoctions helps with pimples because it possesses antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants present in lemon grass also help to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to acne formation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon grass soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples. By tackling multiple factors of acne at once, lemon grass decoctions provide a comprehensive approach to addressing pimples and promoting clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a common cause of acne and pimples.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples and acne.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass is rich in antioxidants such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes are used due to their antibacterial properties, which help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Lemongrass roots have anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe redness and swelling associated with pimples, promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried cymbopogon schoenanthus leaves and stems, ensuring minimal contamination.
Measure 15 grams of the dried herb and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 750 milliliters of boiling water in a large glass container, steeping for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the herbal decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator and use within 24 hours, taking 1 teaspoon three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose decoctions helps with pimples because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's antiseptic qualities help to reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, thereby preventing breakouts. remedy_recipe: Measure 10 grams of dried rosa centifolia petals and place them in a clean glass container.
Add one liter of boiling water to the container, covering the herbs completely, then let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature, then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Use the cooled decoction as a face toner by applying it with a cotton pad or swab, avoiding eye contact and sensitive areas.

Rhyzomes: Contain starches that can be converted into soothing topical treatments to calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Collect 100 grams of dried taraxacum kok saghyz root, finely chopped to release its medicinal properties.
Measure 500 milliliters of distilled water and pour it over the chopped root in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture gently on low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Let the decoction cool and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before use as needed.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves or use dried ones in the ratio of one part herb to two parts water.
Combine the herb mixture with boiling water and let it steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before transferring it to an amber glass bottle for storage in the refrigerator.

remedy_recipe: Rinse 2 teaspoons of dried green tea leaves in cold water to remove any impurities.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pimples with herbal decoctions is to prepare a concoction of calendula, chamomile, and tea tree oil in warm water. Repeat this process twice daily to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pimples that are treated with herbal decoctions are acne, eczema, and rosacea.
